zh.lifeSummary: 當Raoul de BEAUMONT於1171年出生在法國薩特勒 曼斯時，他的父親Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont34歲，母親Lucie de l'Aigle33歲。他於大約1227年與Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1239年4月13日在法國曼恩享年68歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特米雷--哈尼。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: BEAUMONT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Raoul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Raoul de BEAUMONT was born in 1171, in Le Mans, Sarthe, France, his father, Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont, was 34 and his mother, Lucie de l'Aigle, was 33. He married Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE about 1227, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 13 April 1239, in Maine, France, at the age of 68, and was buried in Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Raoul de BEAUMONT est né en 1171, à Le Mans, Sarthe, France, son père, Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Lucie de l'Aigle, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE environ 1227, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 avril 1239, à Maine, France, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

Er hat um 1227 in Frankreich Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 13. April 1239 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Maine, Frankreich und wurde in Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Raoul de BEAUMONTが1171年にフランスサルスル マンスで生まれた時，父Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumontは34歳，母Lucie de l'Aigleは33歳であった。約1227年にフランスでAgnès Émeline de La FLÈCHEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1239年4月13日にフランスメインで68歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスケミールe-エン-チャーニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Raoul de BEAUMONT(이)가 1171년 프랑스 사르테 일 먼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucie de l'Aigle(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1227년 프랑스에서 Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1239년 4월 13일 프랑스 맹에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 체미e--처니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Raoul de BEAUMONT nasceu em 1171, em Le Mans, Sarthe, França, seu pai, Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Lucie de l'Aigle, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE aproximadamente 1227, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 13 de abril de 1239, em Maine, França, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Raoul de BEAUMONT родился в 1171 г. в местности л манс, сарт, Франция, его отцу по имени Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Lucie de l'Aigle, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE около 1227 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 апреля 1239 г. в местности меин, Франция в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности чемирe-ен-чарни, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Raoul de BEAUMONT nació en 1171, en Le Mans, Sarthe, Francia, su papá, Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Lucie de l'Aigle, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Agnès Émeline de La FLÈCHE aproximadamente 1227, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 13 de abril de 1239, en Maine, Francia, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
